text.title: Core VUI Design Principles Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules anchoring the core-vui-design-principles methodology, each grounded in a source.

rule.statement: A spoken response MUST convey a single primary idea; multi-idea responses MUST be split or offered as branching follow-ups.
rule.rationale: Working memory in spoken-mode is shorter than visual; multi-idea responses lose listeners after ~15 seconds.

rule.statement: A single uninterrupted spoken response MUST NOT exceed ~15 seconds; longer content MUST be chunked with `Would you like to hear more?` style branches.
rule.rationale: User attention drops sharply past 15 sec; chunking with consent preserves engagement.

rule.statement: Runtime MUST honour barge-in (user can interrupt mid-prompt); silent forced-listen prompts are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Forced listen breaks turn-taking convention and frustrates frequent users.

rule.statement: Multi-turn dialogs MUST declare an explicit context window (turns remembered + duration); silent reset is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Users build mental models around remembered context; silent reset breaks them.

rule.statement: On error retry, the prompt MUST vary (rephrase + add example) — verbatim repeat is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Verbatim repeat sounds robotic and rarely fixes the underlying confusion.

rule.statement: If the Skip-If preconditions hold, the methodology MUST be skipped and the caller routed to the linked alternative.
rule.rationale: Applying this machinery to the wrong workflow burns tokens without producing a usable artefact.
